sessions.intent: Architect Piece 1: Identity Module for runtime2 builder v2

before.assumptions: Runtime2 has IDataSource, MemoryStack with lazy resolvers, module pattern with [Action] handlers. No identity module exists yet.
before.risk: Low — identity is standalone, no dependencies on other new pieces.

sessions.plan: Identity module with 6 actions (create, get, getAll, archive, setDefault, export). IdentityVariable type with [Sensitive] on PrivateKey, ToString()=PublicKey. Storage via System.DataSource table 'identity'. Lazy %MyIdentity% resolver on System MemoryStack. Per-actor variables: %MyIdentity% (System), %Identity% (User), %ServiceIdentity% (Service). New [Sensitive] attribute + SensitivePropertyFilter for two-mode serialization. Ed25519 key generation via NSec, internal to module.

actions.context: Full architecture plan for identity module, designed interactively with Ingi

after.health: Plan approved, branch created, ready for test-designer
after.notes: Key design decisions: per-actor identity (not shared), [Sensitive] attribute for two-mode serialization, System.DataSource storage, lazy auto-create on %MyIdentity% access

sessions.intent: Create test stubs for Identity module (handlers, IdentityVariable, MyIdentity resolver, [Sensitive] attribute, PLang integration)

before.assumptions: Identity module follows settings module pattern — DataSource storage, MemoryStack resolver, handler CRUD actions. [Sensitive] is new infrastructure.
before.risk: Low — test stubs only, no production code

sessions.plan: Write 40 test stubs across 4 C# test files and 8 PLang test goal files covering identity CRUD, variable type, lazy resolver, sensitive attribute filtering, and full-pipeline PLang integration.

actions.context: C# test stubs following SettingsDataTests pattern — engine setup/teardown, Assert.Fail bodies
PLang integration test stubs — Start goal, throw 'not implemented' bodies

after.health: 48 test stubs created (40 C# + 8 PLang). Two open questions flagged for architect.
after.notes: Questions: (1) duplicate name when archived — error or re-create? (2) PrivateKey dot navigation — blocked or serialization-only?

sessions.intent: Resolve test-designer open questions, add rename + unarchive actions

before.assumptions: Test-designer flagged two design questions. Ingi will confirm direction.
before.risk: Low — design decisions only, no code changes.

sessions.plan: Resolve both questions: (1) duplicate name = always error, add unarchive + rename actions. (2) [Sensitive] is serialization only, dot navigation works. Update plan to v2 with new actions and corrected test expectations.

actions.context: Summary of test-designer questions and architect decisions
Updated plan with 8 actions (was 6), 59 tests (was 48), resolved open questions

after.health: v2 plan ready. Two new actions (unarchive, rename), one test renamed, 11 new tests specified. Ready for test-designer to update stubs, then coder.
after.notes: Ingi confirmed both decisions. Names are identities — never reuse across archived. [Sensitive] is serialization boundary, not access control.

sessions.intent: Implement identity module: 8 actions, [Sensitive] infrastructure, MyIdentity resolver, 49 C# tests

before.assumptions: Architect v2 plan is final. Test stubs exist. NSec is already referenced. Settings module provides the DataSource pattern to follow.
before.risk: Medium — new module with new infrastructure ([Sensitive]), but follows established patterns.

sessions.plan: Phase 1: [Sensitive] infrastructure (SensitiveAttribute, SensitivePropertyFilter, wire into JsonStreamSerializer). Phase 2: Identity types (IdentityVariable with persistence, KeyGenerator with Ed25519). Phase 3: IdentityData on Actor + %MyIdentity% DynamicData registration. Phase 4: 8 identity handlers (create, get, getAll, archive, unarchive, rename, setDefault, export). Phase 5: Implement 49 C# test bodies. Phase 6: PLang test goals (mark pending — need builder prompt update).

actions.context: Phase 2-3: Identity types with OBP persistence, Ed25519 key generation, lazy IdentityData resolver
Phase 3: Add lazy Identity property + register %MyIdentity% DynamicData on MemoryStack
Phase 4: 8 identity CRUD handlers following settings module pattern
Phase 5: Implemented all 51 C# test bodies — all pass
Fixed pre-existing namespace collision: global using Serializers shadowed by using directive

after.health: All production code implemented, all 51 C# tests pass (1645/1645 total suite). PLang tests pending builder prompt update.
after.notes: Also fixed pre-existing SerializerRegistryTests.cs namespace collision. Identity module follows settings module pattern exactly.

sessions.intent: Analyze identity module code for OBP compliance, simplification, readability, behavioral reasoning, and deletion tests

before.assumptions: Coder implemented 8 handlers, IdentityVariable, IdentityData, KeyGenerator, SensitivePropertyFilter, and Actor.Identity. 51 C# tests pass.
before.risk: Low — analysis only, no code changes.

sessions.plan: 5-pass analysis: (1) OBP compliance, (2) Simplification, (3) Readability, (4) Behavioral reasoning, (5) Deletion tests. Focus on identity module files + infrastructure changes.

actions.context: 5-pass analysis of 15 files. Found 1 behavioral bug (get.cs overwrites MyIdentity on by-name fetch), duplicate auto-create, non-atomic rename, 2 test gaps, 2 minor cleanups.

after.health: Analysis complete. Verdict: FAIL. 1 bug, 4 needs-work items, 2 test gaps.
after.notes: Critical: get.cs line 24 calls Identity.Update() on by-name fetch, corrupting %MyIdentity%. Recommend sending back to coder.

sessions.intent: Test quality analysis of identity module implementation (coder v1)

before.assumptions: Coder reports 51 C# tests pass, 1645 total suite. PLang tests are stubs. Need to verify test honesty and coverage.
before.risk: Low — read-only analysis, no code changes.

sessions.plan: Run full test suite, extract coverage, analyze test quality (weak assertions, false greens, missing edge cases, deletion test), check PLang test status, write test-report.json with findings and verdict.

actions.context: Test quality report: 8 findings (4 major, 4 minor), verdict needs-fixes
Cobertura XML coverage output from TUnit --coverage

after.health: Analysis complete. Verdict: needs-fixes. 3 major findings require coder action.
after.notes: Key gaps: export default path untested (60% line), two weak error key assertions, all PLang tests are stubs.

sessions.intent: Re-review coder v2 fixes for identity module

before.assumptions: Coder v2 addressed all 7 findings from analyzer v1. Need to verify fixes are correct and check for regressions.
before.risk: Low — focused re-review of fix-introduced code only.

sessions.plan: Verify each of 7 fixes: trace by-name path, check GetOrCreateDefaultAsync edge cases, verify rename atomicity, validate new tests.

actions.context: Re-review of 4 changed files. All 7 original fixes correct. 1 new finding: GetOrCreateDefaultAsync doesn't check SaveAsync result.

after.health: Analysis complete. Verdict: FAIL (soft). 1 medium-severity regression in consolidated method.
after.notes: GetOrCreateDefaultAsync line 88 doesn't check SaveAsync result. Original Get.Run() did check it. Quick fix.

sessions.intent: Fix GetOrCreateDefaultAsync SaveAsync result check (code analyzer v2 finding)

before.assumptions: Single medium-severity finding: SaveAsync result not checked in GetOrCreateDefaultAsync. Quick fix.
before.risk: Low — small change, throw on save failure.

sessions.plan: Add SaveAsync result check in types.cs (throw InvalidOperationException on failure). Add try/catch in get.cs to convert to Data.FromError. IdentityData.cs unchanged — throw propagates naturally.

actions.context: Check SaveAsync result, throw on failure to prevent phantom identity
Catch InvalidOperationException from GetOrCreateDefaultAsync, return Data.FromError

after.notes: Throw on save failure restores the error check that existed in v1. Both callers handle it correctly.
